title: US Sanctions 29 Shadow Fleet Vessels Transporting Iranian Oil
detail: The US Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control sanctions 29 shadow fleet vessels and their management firms that have transported hundreds of millions of dollars' worth of Iranian petroleum products. The action also targets an Egyptian businessman whose companies are associated with seven of the vessels, plus several shipping companies. The designations bring the total vessels sanctioned as part of the maximum pressure campaign to over 180 since January, crippling Iran's ability to sell oil at market prices.

media.caption: US sanctions 29 shadow fleet vessels transporting hundreds of millions in Iranian petroleum

title: Shadow Fleet Crackdown Tightens Squeeze on Iranian Oil Revenue
detail: The latest round of shadow fleet sanctions is expected to further reduce Iran's crude exports, already estimated at 800,000 barrels per day, down from 1.3 million bpd before the snapback. China, Iran's largest remaining customer, has leveraged Iran's isolation to demand deeper discounts of $10-15 per barrel below market rates. The State Department frames the action as part of National Security Presidential Memorandum 2, targeting Iran's 'shadow banking, money laundering, weapons proliferation, and sanctions evasion networks.'

media.caption: Shadow fleet crackdown squeezes Iran's crude exports and forces deeper discounts to China

title: OFAC Targets Networks Supplying Iran's Missile and Weapons Programs
detail: Alongside the shadow fleet designations, OFAC sanctions over 30 individuals and entities enabling Iran's ballistic missile and advanced conventional weapons production. The action targets procurement networks that have been attempting to source replacement components for missile systems and centrifuge equipment destroyed in the June strikes. The sanctions aim to prevent Iran from rebuilding the weapons infrastructure that took decades to develop.

media.caption: OFAC targets networks supplying Iran's ballistic missile and weapons programs
